header_summary:
The recent release of Bitcoin Core 29.3 introduces significant enhancements, including bug fixes, performance improvements, and the addition of new features such as updates to the peer-to-peer network and wallet functionality. This version, which supports Linux Kernel 3.17+, macOS 13+, and Windows 10+, continues to back old wallet versions and emphasizes the project's commitment to security, usability, and performance improvements. Users are encouraged to transition from older versions by shutting them down completely before installation, with the note that direct updates from EOL versions are supported but might be delayed if data directory migration is necessary ([Source](https://gnusha.org/pi/bitcoindev/CAO3Wf41sAHeZ-KR1mp=xNuydstcMk9R93ou5Jobmq9ngn7TKXA@mail.gmail.com/T/#u#m110222ead81e7ac2f1becbfe3e4f49db3200dafc)).

In addressing the challenges associated with wallet fund recovery, Bitcoin Improvement Proposal 392 (BIP392) for Silent Payment Output Script Descriptors proposes adding annotations to descriptor expressions. This aims to include essential metadata for efficient fund recovery directly within the descriptor, thereby reducing the resource-intensive scanning requirements currently faced during the wallet recovery process. The proposed annotations are a step towards simplifying fund recovery without altering the produced scripts, reflecting a blend of practicality and innovation in enhancing Bitcoin's usability ([Source](https://github.com/craigraw/bips/blob/descriptorannotations/bip-descriptorannotations.mediawiki)).

Furthermore, the Hourglass proposal has been revised to limit the Bitcoin amount that can be spent from P2PK outputs to one Bitcoin per block, aiming to mitigate risks associated with mass liquidations and ensure network stability. This revision, documented in the Hourglass Proposal V2, underlines the Bitcoin community's proactive stance in addressing potential vulnerabilities through technical adjustments and collaborative feedback ([Source](https://github.com/cryptoquick/bips/blob/hourglass-v2/bip-hourglass-v2.mediawiki)). Additionally, Bitcoin PIPEs version 2 introduces an innovative method for enforcing spending conditions using cryptographic techniques, specifically witness encryption, allowing for more expressive on-chain policies without altering consensus rules. This advancement highlights ongoing efforts to expand Bitcoin's functionality while maintaining its core security principles, despite computational challenges associated with witness encryption ([Source](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/bitcoin-pipes-v2/2249)).

Lastly, running a Bitcoin node is emphasized as a crucial measure for verifying the authenticity of Bitcoin transactions independently, enhancing privacy, and avoiding potential financial losses due to reliance on third-party services, as illustrated by the confusion during the 2017 split with Bitcoin Cash. This underscores the importance of direct transaction verification in safeguarding assets and maintaining privacy within the Bitcoin network ([Source](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/what-is-the-importance-of-running-a-node/2079/15)).

recent_posts.title: Bitcoin Core v29.3 Released
recent_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin Core 29.3 is now downloadable, offering enhancements and bug fixes.
- It supports updates from EOL versions and works on recent major OS platforms.
- New features include P2P network changes and wallet functionality improvements.

recent_posts.title: [BIP Proposal] Output Script Descriptor Annotations
recent_posts.summary:
- The BIP392 proposal identifies a recovery issue in wallet funds for Silent Payment Output Script Descriptors.
- It suggests adding metadata annotations to descriptors to improve fund recovery efficiency.
- These annotations include essential recovery information like starting block height and gap limit.

recent_posts.title: Hourglass V2 Update
recent_posts.summary:
- The Hourglass proposal has been revised to limit Bitcoin spends to one per block for security.
- Details of the revised Hourglass proposal are accessible on GitHub for in-depth review.
- Community feedback is sought to refine the proposal further, exemplifying collaborative development.

recent_posts.title: Bitcoin PIPEs v2
recent_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin PIPEs v2 introduces spending conditions on the blockchain using witness encryption.
- It enables conditional signatures without altering Bitcoin’s consensus rules or security model.
- Despite computational challenges, this approach promises more expressive, secure Bitcoin transactions.

recent_posts.title: What is the importance of running a node?
recent_posts.summary:
- Running a node verifies Bitcoin authenticity, reducing reliance on third-party attestations.
- The 2017 split with Bitcoin Cash showcased the risks of non-full-node client reliance.
- Enhancing privacy and confidential transactions are secondary benefits of running a node.

recent_posts.title: Recent OP_RETURN output statistics
recent_posts.summary:
- Analysis between blocks 915800 and 936000 shows 24,362,310 transactions with OP_RETURN outputs.
- A transaction had 91 OP_RETURN outputs; 10% of transactions contain more than 10kB of data.
- 34,283 transactions burned a total of 1,463,488 satoshis through OP_RETURN outputs.

active_posts.title: Algorithm Agility for Bitcoin to maintain security in the face of quantum and classic breaks in the signature algorithms
active_posts.summary:
- The proposal outlines enhancing Bitcoin's security against advanced threats using algorithm agility.
- It suggests a dual signature system with primary and emergency algorithms for flexibility.
- The aim is to maintain Bitcoin's reliability as a long-term investment by adapting to evolving cryptographic challenges.

active_posts.title: Addressing remaining points on BIP 54
active_posts.summary:
- Luke Dashjr criticizes BIP 54 for its nLockTime usage, affecting ASIC optimization.
- Jeremy Rubin suggests an alternative to restricting transaction sizes with a new sparse Merkle tree.
- The debate leans towards practical solutions, avoiding major protocol changes and ecosystem risks.

active_posts.title: Bip54: add sequence, timestamp (and version) to GBT
active_posts.summary:
- BIP54 focuses on setting `nLockTime` and `nSequence` in coinbase transactions by mining pools.
- It introduces keys like `coinbase_locktime` and `coinbase_sequence` to the `getblocktemplate` for pre-emptive compliance.
- Efforts aim to future-proof transactions against protocol changes, with details on GitHub and the BIP54 document.

active_posts.title: Gossip Observer: New project to monitor the Lightning P2P network
active_posts.summary:
- The project aims to analyze the Lightning gossip network, decreasing message convergence delay.
- Significant findings include inefficiencies in network connectivity and selective filtering policies.
- Future goals involve expanding infrastructure and engaging the community for further study.

active_posts.title: Major BIP 360 Update
active_posts.summary:
- Ethan Heilman and colleagues have completely rewritten BIP 360 for clarity and risk mitigation.
- The revision introduces "Pay-to-Tapscript-Hash" to protect against quantum vulnerabilities, not adding post-quantum signatures.
- Enhanced with Python and Rust test vectors, the updated BIP 360 seeks community review to advance Bitcoin's quantum resistance.

active_posts.title: SHRINCS: 324-byte stateful post-quantum signatures with static backups
active_posts.summary:
- SHRINCS merges stateful and stateless hash-based signatures for efficiency and robustness.
- It uses an unbalanced XMSS tree and SPHINCS+ variant, allowing seamless mode switching.
- The scheme optimizes for scenarios with few signatures needed, ensuring security through flexible state management.

today_in_history_posts.title: Request for a new BIP number (and discussion): Improved HD wallet generation.
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Jona has developed a new HD wallet methodology, modifying BIP32.
- He seeks feedback and assistance to enhance the proposal's security.
- Jona also requests guidance on drafting the proposal into a BIP if appropriate.

today_in_history_posts.title: Routing and explicit trust
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- The paper queries "channel counterparty" and "clearinghouse" risks and their trustless routing.
- Trust in level 2 protocols requires trusting the counterparty to a limit, mitigating some risk.
- Systematic risks and interconnected counterparty failures highlight challenges in exposure diversification.

today_in_history_posts.title: An Onchain Implementation Of Mining Feerate Futures
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Mining fee futures address blockchain onchain fee unpredictability, stabilizing miner income.
- A binary contract with blockchain users and miners sets payments based on fee fluctuations.
- This scheme reduces risks from fee changes, aiding both users and miners.
